title: Submit an adult passport application in Canada and pay the fees - Canada.ca
content:
Apply for a new adult passport in Canada

## Submit your application and pay the fees
Where are you right now? Canada | US | Outside Canada and the US

## Passport and travel document service fees have increased
As of March 31, 2026 , most fees for passports and travel document services have increased. Learn how these changes may affect your application .
How you submit your application and what fees you pay depend on when you need the passport.
- Your passport will be mailed to you unless you pay the extra fee to pick it up in person (pick-up service).
- Some in-person locations can process your application quicker than others, and some offer express or urgent service for even faster processing.
Tell us when you need the passport. You’ll learn how and where to submit it.

## You need your passport in 10 to 20 business days or more and want to apply in person

### Submit your application
If you’re applying in person and need your passport in 10-20 business days
You can book an appointment online (opens in a new tab) .
Some locations have a high demand for appointments. Their availability may vary.
Without an appointment (walk-in)
You can get walk-in services at a Service Canada Centre or a passport office (opens in a new tab) .
Wait times may be long at some passport offices. If the location is busy, we may prioritize people who need a passport within 48 hours.
Someone else can submit your application for you if you can’t go yourself. Learn more about who can submit a passport application on your behalf (opens in a new tab) .
Your fees depend on what services you’re getting. How you can pay them depends on how you’re applying.
Make sure you pay the correct fees, listed below.
5-year adult passport (age 16 and up)
[Error loading fee. Check the fee list .]
10-year adult passport (age 16 and up)
[Error loading fee. Check the fee list .]
There are other fees for
- standard pickup at a passport office
- replacing a lost or stolen passport that’s still valid
- other administrative services
There’s no fee for having your passport mailed to you.
Standard pickup (on or after 10 business days)
[Error loading fee. Check the fee list .]
Additional fee for replacing a lost or stolen passport that was still valid You must pay this fee if you report a passport as lost or stolen and are applying for a replacement. This fee does not include the cost of the new passport.
Additional fee for replacing a lost or stolen passport that was still valid
You must pay this fee if you report a passport as lost or stolen and are applying for a replacement. This fee does not include the cost of the new passport.
[Error loading fee. Check the fee list .]
How to pay your fees in person
You can pay using any of the following:
- credit card or prepaid card (Visa ® , MasterCard ® or American Express ® )
- certified cheque or money order (postal or bank) in Canadian funds with the exact amount
- with the exact amount
- cheque from Canadian provincial, territorial or federal government departments For example, the Ministry of Social Services.
- For example, the Ministry of Social Services.
All cheques must be made payable to the Receiver General for Canada.
We don’t accept cash or personal cheques.
You can’t pay online for in-Canada services.
You won’t get a refund for the service fee if you:
- cancel your application
- are refused a travel document
- are issued a passport for a shorter validity period than requested For example, if we issue a passport that’s valid for less than 5 years, despite you applying for a 5- or 10-year passport, you won’t get a refund. This may happen if you’ve lost or damaged your passport multiple times.
- For example, if we issue a passport that’s valid for less than 5 years, despite you applying for a 5- or 10-year passport, you won’t get a refund. This may happen if you’ve lost or damaged your passport multiple times.
- This may happen if you’ve lost or damaged your passport multiple times.
Only the consular fees (opens in a new tab) , which apply to all adult applications, are refundable if a passport is not issued.

## You need your passport in 20 business days or more and want to apply by mail
Submit the application by mail from Alberta, Manitoba, Ontario, Quebec or Saskatchewan
Use a certified courier or traceable mail service to reduce the delivery time of your application and help protect your documents.
Courier mailing address
Government of Canada Passport Program 22 De Varennes Street Gatineau, Quebec  J8T 8R1
Non-courier mailing address
Government of Canada Passport Program Gatineau, Quebec  K1A 0G3
Submit the application by mail from British Columbia, Northwest Territories, Nunavut or Yukon
Use a certified courier or traceable mail service to reduce the delivery time of your application and help protect your documents.
Courier mailing address
Government of Canada Passport Program 22 De Varennes Street Gatineau, Quebec  J8T 8R1
Non-courier mailing address
Government of Canada Passport Program PO Box 4000 STN LCD 3 Mississauga, Ontario  L5K 0A9
Submit the application by mail from New Brunswick, Newfoundland and Labrador, Nova Scotia or Prince Edward Island
Use a certified courier or traceable mail service to reduce the delivery time of your application and help protect your documents.
Government of Canada Passport Program 22 De Varennes Street Gatineau, Quebec  J8T 8R1
Non-courier mailing address
Government of Canada Passport Program Gatineau, Quebec  K1A 0G3
Your fees depend on what services you’re getting. How you can pay them depends on how you’re applying.
Make sure you pay the correct fees, listed below.
5-year adult passport (age 16 and up)
[Error loading fee. Check the fee list .]
10-year adult passport (age 16 and up)
[Error loading fee. Check the fee list .]
There’s another fee if you’re replacing a lost or stolen passport that’s still valid.
Additional fee for replacing a lost or stolen passport that was still valid You must pay this fee if you report a passport as lost or stolen and are applying for a replacement. This fee does not include the cost of the new passport.
Additional fee for replacing a lost or stolen passport that was still valid
You must pay this fee if you report a passport as lost or stolen and are applying for a replacement. This fee does not include the cost of the new passport.
[Error loading fee. Check the fee list .]
How to pay your fees by mail
Fill out the payment section of your application form with any of the following:
- a credit card (Visa ® , MasterCard ® or American Express ® )
- an embossed prepaid card that has raised letters or numbers (Visa ® , MasterCard ® or American Express ® )
- a certified cheque or money order (postal or bank) in Canadian funds with the exact amount
- with the exact amount
- a cheque from Canadian provincial, territorial or federal government departments (for example, the Ministry of Social Services) All cheques must be made payable to the Receiver General for Canada.
- All cheques must be made payable to the Receiver General for Canada.
Once you’ve mailed us your application, you can’t change the method of payment .
You cannot pay for your application
- with cash or personal cheques
You won’t get a refund for the service fee if you:
- cancel your application
- are refused a travel document
- are issued a passport for a shorter validity period than requested For example, if we issue a passport that’s valid for less than 5 years, despite you applying for a 5- or 10-year passport, you won’t get a refund. This may happen if you’ve lost or damaged your passport multiple times.
- For example, if we issue a passport that’s valid for less than 5 years, despite you applying for a 5- or 10-year passport, you won’t get a refund. This may happen if you’ve lost or damaged your passport multiple times.
- This may happen if you’ve lost or damaged your passport multiple times.
Only the consular fees (opens in a new tab) , which apply to all adult applications, are refundable if a passport is not issued.

## You need your passport in 2 to 9 business days (express pickup)

### Submit your application
If you’re applying in person and need your passport in 2 to 9 business days
You may need to provide proof that you need the passport .
You can book an appointment online (opens in a new tab) .
Some locations have a high demand for appointments. Their availability may vary.
Without an appointment (walk-in)
Go to a passport office with express pick-up service (opens in a new tab) .
If the location is busy, we may prioritize people who need a passport within 48 hours.
Your fees depend on what services you’re getting. How you can pay them depends on how you’re applying.

- are issued a passport for a shorter validity period than requested For example, if we issue a passport that’s valid for less than 5 years, despite you applying for a 5- or 10-year passport, you won’t get a refund. This may happen if you’ve lost or damaged your passport multiple times.
- For example, if we issue a passport that’s valid for less than 5 years, despite you applying for a 5- or 10-year passport, you won’t get a refund. This may happen if you’ve lost or damaged your passport multiple times.

## You need your passport by the end of the next business day (urgent pickup)

### Submit your application
If you need your passport by end of next business day
- Go to a passport office with urgent pick-up (opens in a new tab) . You do not need an appointment. You must provide proof that you need the passport .
- You do not need an appointment.
- You must provide proof that you need the passport .
- Make sure it’s a passport office and not a regular Service Canada Centre or you won’t get your passport in time. You can check walk-in wait times (opens in a new tab) before you go.
- You can check walk-in wait times (opens in a new tab) before you go.
Your fees depend on what services you’re getting. How you can pay them depends on how you’re applying.
Make sure you pay the correct fees, listed below.

## You need a passport on a weekend or statutory holiday (emergencies only)

### Submit your application
Call us ahead of time at 1-800-567-6868 to find out how to submit the application. We answer the phone Monday to Friday
- from 8:30 am to 5 pm (your local time)
- from 9:00 am to 5:30 pm (Newfoundland)
- except for statutory holidays
You must provide proof you need the passport .
You can leave a message and ask for us to call you back
- on Friday or a weekday before a statutory holiday after 5 pm (your local time), 5:30 pm (Newfoundland)
- on Saturday or Sunday
We’ll call you with information on emergency services between 9 am and 5 pm (your local time).
To get the weekend or statutory holiday service, you need to pay an additional [Error loading fee. Check the fee list .] on top of the regular passport fees.
If you need the weekend or holiday service for your family members at the same time, you need to pay an urgent service fee of [Error loading fee. Check the fee list .] per family member .
For example, if you apply for a 10-year passport for yourself and a 5-year passport for your child, the fees will be
- [Error loading fee. Check the fee list .] for your passport
- [Error loading fee. Check the fee list .] for your weekend or statutory holiday service
- [Error loading fee. Check the fee list .] for the child’s passport
- [Error loading fee. Check the fee list .] for the child’s urgent service
5-year adult passport (age 16 and up)
[Error loading fee. Check the fee list .]
10-year adult passport (age 16 and up)
[Error loading fee. Check the fee list .]
Child passport (age 0 to 15)
[Error loading fee. Check the fee list .]
There are other fees for
- replacing a lost or stolen passport
- other administrative services
Weekend or statutory holiday service This service is only for emergencies and if you have to travel over that specific weekend or statutory holiday
Weekend or statutory holiday service
This service is only
- if you have to travel over that specific weekend or statutory holiday

How to pay your fees in person
You can pay using any of the following:
- credit card or prepaid card (Visa ® , MasterCard ® or American Express ® )
- certified cheque or money order (postal or bank) in Canadian funds with the exact amount
- with the exact amount
- cheque from Canadian provincial, territorial or federal government departments For example, the Ministry of Social Services.
- For example, the Ministry of Social Services.
All cheques must be made payable to the Receiver General for Canada.
We don’t accept cash or personal cheques.
You can’t pay online for in-Canada services.
You won’t get a refund for the service fee if you
- cancel your application
- are refused a travel document
- are issued a passport for a shorter validity period than requested For example, if we issue a passport that’s valid for less than 5 years, despite you applying for a 5- or 10-year passport, you won’t get a refund. This may happen if you’ve lost or damaged your passport multiple times.
- For example, if we issue a passport that’s valid for less than 5 years, despite you applying for a 5- or 10-year passport, you won’t get a refund. This may happen if you’ve lost or damaged your passport multiple times.
- This may happen if you’ve lost or damaged your passport multiple times.
Only the consular fees (opens in a new tab) , which apply to all adult applications, are refundable if a passport is not issued.
Proof you need the passport can be
- an airline, bus or train ticket If travelling by car, a written statement explaining why you need to travel
- If travelling by car, a written statement explaining why you need to travel
- a travel itinerary showing proof of payment
- proof of illness or death in the family requiring you to travel
- a written statement from you or a third party explaining why you need the passport For example, for legal, banking or residency requirements
- For example, for legal, banking or residency requirements
If we can’t deliver the passport in time to you or your child, we aren’t liable for any losses.
We recommend that you don’t finalize any travel plans until you get your passport.
Passport offices and Service Canada Centres will be closed on these dates in 2024 :
- June 24 (Quebec only)
- August 5 (except Quebec)
